findings.message: New Dashboard view introduces a fetch/API path to /internal/summary that ships with no RUM or error instrumentation reachable from it. The fetched-summary behavior (and its load failures) is entirely unobserved — no vital, error, or view-level RUM signal is emitted for this user-facing code path.
findings.suggestion: Instrument this view: emit a RUM signal on render/fetch and, on failure, route the error into the js-errors counter (e.g. report via the RUM error path) so the view's load behavior is observable.

findings.message: The fetch chain (.then(r => r.json()).then(setData)) has no .catch. A failed fetch or JSON parse rejects silently — the error is swallowed and never reaches the js-errors counter, so the failure is invisible. Relying on the global unhandledrejection listener is fragile and yields no signal tied to this view.
findings.suggestion: Add a .catch that surfaces the failure into RUM/the js-errors counter (and renders an error state), mirroring the .catch(() => setError(true)) pattern used in Items.tsx, so the failure is observed rather than swallowed.
